epics.title: [Epic] Dragon Idea Engine Master Codex
epics.userStory: As a platform architect, I want a master codex that defines the platform, agents, and governance model, so that the platform has a shared implementation and governance blueprint.
epics.summary:
Dragon Idea Engine is an **autonomous software factory** designed to turn ideas into fully developed software projects using modular AI agents.

The system:
acceptanceCriteria: All major implementation slices for this codex section are represented by child stories.
The epic outcome is documented clearly enough to guide implementation and review.
Known dependencies, sequencing constraints, and governance concerns are captured.

technicalDetails: Idea → Architecture → Repo → Issues → Code → Review → Deploy → Feedback

childStories.userStory: As a platform architect, I want the system architecture capability, so that multiple runners can exist.
childStories.description:
Multiple runners can exist.

Example:
acceptanceCriteria: The System Architecture behavior is implemented according to the codex definition.
The implementation covers these codex details: Dragon Idea Engine UI, Dragon API Controller, RabbitMQ.
Dependencies and integration points with the rest of Dragon Idea Engine Master Codex are documented.

technicalDetails: Dragon Agent Runner                Dragon Agent Runner
devNotes.rawReference:
```
                Dragon Idea Engine UI
                        │
                        ▼
                Dragon API Controller
                        │
                        ▼
                    RabbitMQ
                        │
       ┌────────────────┴─────────────────┐
       │                                  │
Dragon Agent Runner                Dragon Agent Runner
   (worker)                            (worker)
       │                                  │
       ▼                                  ▼
  Agent Plugins                       Agent Plugins
```

Multiple runners can exist.

Example:

```
10+ workers
100+ concurrent jobs
```

```

run CLI commands
connect to RabbitMQ
execute queued jobs
return results
```

```
dragon-agent-runner developer --repo crm --issue 42
```

Service mode:

```
dragon-agent-runner --service
```

epics.title: [Epic] Dragon Idea Engine Infrastructure Architecture
epics.userStory: As a platform operator, I want a deployment architecture for Dragon Idea Engine, so that the platform can run locally on Raspberry Pi and scale cleanly later.
epics.summary:
This section defines the **deployment model and repository layout** for the Dragon Idea Engine platform.

The system is designed to operate in two phases:
acceptanceCriteria: All major implementation slices for this codex section are represented by child stories.
The epic outcome is documented clearly enough to guide implementation and review.
Known dependencies, sequencing constraints, and governance concerns are captured.

childStories.heading: Deployment Philosophy
childStories.title: [Story] Dragon Idea Engine Infrastructure Architecture: Deployment Philosophy
childStories.userStory: As a platform operator, I want the deployment philosophy capability, so that dragon Idea Engine prioritizes: Primary target platform:.
childStories.description:
Dragon Idea Engine prioritizes:

Primary target platform:
acceptanceCriteria: The Deployment Philosophy behavior is implemented according to the codex definition.
The implementation covers these codex details: self-hosted, portable, low-power infrastructure.
Dependencies and integration points with the rest of Dragon Idea Engine Infrastructure Architecture are documented.

devNotes.rawReference:
Dragon Idea Engine prioritizes:

```
self-hosted
portable
low-power infrastructure
edge deployment
distributed scalability
```

Primary target platform:

```
Raspberry Pi 5
```

Recommended configuration:

```
8GB RAM
NVMe SSD via PCIe
Ubuntu Server
Docker
```

---

childStories.heading: Phase 1 — Raspberry Pi Autonomous Engine
childStories.title: [Story] Dragon Idea Engine Infrastructure Architecture: Phase 1 Raspberry Pi Autonomous Engine
childStories.userStory: As a platform operator, I want the phase 1 raspberry pi autonomous engine capability, so that phase 1 runs **all components on a single device**.
childStories.description:
Phase 1 runs **all components on a single device**.

Architecture:
acceptanceCriteria: The Phase 1 Raspberry Pi Autonomous Engine behavior is implemented according to the codex definition.
The implementation covers these codex details: RabbitMQ Queue, Agent Runner Workers, Agent Plugins.
Dependencies and integration points with the rest of Dragon Idea Engine Infrastructure Architecture are documented.

devNotes.rawReference:
Phase 1 runs **all components on a single device**.

Architecture:

```
                ┌───────────────────────┐
                │ Dragon UI (React)     │
                └─────────────┬─────────┘
                              │
                              ▼
                      ┌───────────────┐
                      │ Dragon API    │
                      └───────┬───────┘
                              │
                              ▼
                      ┌───────────────┐
                      │ Orchestrator  │
                      └───────┬───────┘
                              │
                              ▼
                        RabbitMQ Queue
                              │
                              ▼
                     Agent Runner Workers
                              │
                              ▼
                        Agent Plugins
```

Everything runs locally.

---

devNotes.rawReference:
Recommended filesystem structure:

```
/dragon-engine
    /config
    /repos
    /workspaces
    /logs
    /agents
    /models
```

```
repos       → cached repositories
workspaces  → temporary agent workspaces
logs        → job and system logs
agents      → plugin agents
models      → local AI models
```

epics.title: [Epic] RISK ANALYSIS AND COMPLIANCE SCORING
epics.userStory: As a product owner, I want risk and compliance scoring before ideas are approved, so that unsafe or non-compliant ideas are filtered before build work starts.
epics.summary:
Before an idea can be approved for development, Dragon Idea Engine must evaluate **legal, regulatory, and ethical risks** associated with the project.

This ensures the system does not automatically generate projects that expose the operator to legal liability.
acceptanceCriteria: All major implementation slices for this codex section are represented by child stories.
The epic outcome is documented clearly enough to guide implementation and review.
Known dependencies, sequencing constraints, and governance concerns are captured.

technicalDetails: Final Score = Idea Score − Risk Penalty

childStories.heading: Risk Score Model
childStories.title: [Story] RISK ANALYSIS AND COMPLIANCE SCORING: Risk Score Model
childStories.userStory: As a product owner, I want the risk score model capability, so that risk scoring works inversely.
childStories.description:
Risk scoring works inversely.

Higher risk reduces the final score.
acceptanceCriteria: The Risk Score Model behavior is implemented according to the codex definition.
The implementation covers these codex details: Risk Penalty = 0 – 40, Final Score = Idea Score − Risk Penalty, Idea Score: 75.
Dependencies and integration points with the rest of RISK ANALYSIS AND COMPLIANCE SCORING are documented.

technicalDetails: Final Score = Idea Score − Risk Penalty
devNotes.rawReference:
Risk scoring works inversely.

Higher risk reduces the final score.

```
Risk Penalty = 0 – 40
```

Final idea score becomes:

```
Final Score = Idea Score − Risk Penalty
```

Example:

```
Idea Score: 75
Risk Penalty: 18
Final Score: 57
Decision: DEFER
```

---

epics.title: [Epic] ETHICS ANALYSIS AGENT
epics.userStory: As a product owner, I want an ethics analysis agent, so that harmful ideas are caught before they enter the build pipeline.
epics.summary:
The **Ethics Analysis Agent** evaluates whether a proposed project could cause **harm, misuse, manipulation, or social risk**.

Legal compliance alone is not sufficient. Many harmful systems are technically legal but ethically questionable.
acceptanceCriteria: All major implementation slices for this codex section are represented by child stories.
The epic outcome is documented clearly enough to guide implementation and review.
Known dependencies, sequencing constraints, and governance concerns are captured.

technicalDetails: Final Score = Idea Score − Risk Penalty − Ethics Penalty

childStories.heading: Ethical Risk Model
childStories.title: [Story] ETHICS ANALYSIS AGENT: Ethical Risk Model
childStories.userStory: As a product owner, I want the ethical risk model capability, so that ethical evaluation produces an **Ethics Risk Score**.
childStories.description:
Ethical evaluation produces an **Ethics Risk Score**.

This penalty is applied alongside the legal risk score.
acceptanceCriteria: The Ethical Risk Model behavior is implemented according to the codex definition.
The implementation covers these codex details: Ethics Penalty = 0 – 25, Final Score = Idea Score − Risk Penalty − Ethics Penalty.
Dependencies and integration points with the rest of ETHICS ANALYSIS AGENT are documented.

technicalDetails: Final Score = Idea Score − Risk Penalty − Ethics Penalty
devNotes.rawReference:
Ethical evaluation produces an **Ethics Risk Score**.

```
Ethics Penalty = 0 – 25
```

This penalty is applied alongside the legal risk score.

```
Final Score = Idea Score − Risk Penalty − Ethics Penalty
```

epics.title: [Epic] REUSABLE COMPONENT LIBRARY
epics.userStory: As a platform operator, I want a reusable component library, so that new projects can be assembled faster with less duplicated code.
epics.summary:
Dragon Idea Engine should avoid repeatedly generating the same infrastructure code across projects.

Instead, it maintains a **Reusable Component Library** consisting of standardized modules and shared services.
acceptanceCriteria: All major implementation slices for this codex section are represented by child stories.
The epic outcome is documented clearly enough to guide implementation and review.
Known dependencies, sequencing constraints, and governance concerns are captured.

The Raspberry Pi edition will provide **shared infrastructure services** running locally.

These services act as **platform primitives** for all generated projects.

Core services include:

```
Database
Messaging
Authentication
Caching
Object Storage
Logging
```

These are deployed once and reused by all projects.

acceptanceCriteria: All major implementation slices for this codex section are represented by child stories.

Known dependencies, sequencing constraints, and governance concerns are captured.

epics.title: [Epic] MANDATORY OPEN SOURCE POLICY
epics.userStory: As a product owner, I want a mandatory open source policy, so that generated projects follow the intended distribution rules.
epics.summary:
All code generated by Dragon Idea Engine, whether for internal testing, projects, or user-submitted ideas, **must be open source**. This ensures:

No project should remain closed source unless explicitly approved under exceptional circumstances with human oversight.
acceptanceCriteria: All major implementation slices for this codex section are represented by child stories.
The epic outcome is documented clearly enough to guide implementation and review.
Known dependencies, sequencing constraints, and governance concerns are captured.

Dependencies and integration points with the rest of MANDATORY OPEN SOURCE POLICY are documented.

- Optional alternatives (configurable per project):

epics.title: [Epic] PROJECT GENERATION PIPELINE
epics.userStory: As a delivery team, I want a project generation pipeline, so that approved ideas can move through a repeatable delivery flow.
epics.summary:
The Project Generation Pipeline defines the automated process that transforms an approved idea into a fully structured project, including repositories, architecture, code, documentation, and deployment configuration.

This pipeline ensures that all projects generated by Dragon Idea Engine follow consistent standards and leverage reusable components wherever possible.
acceptanceCriteria: All major implementation slices for this codex section are represented by child stories.
The epic outcome is documented clearly enough to guide implementation and review.
Known dependencies, sequencing constraints, and governance concerns are captured.

childStories.heading: Pipeline Overview
childStories.title: [Story] PROJECT GENERATION PIPELINE: Pipeline Overview
childStories.userStory: As a delivery team, I want the pipeline overview capability, so that the pipeline consists of sequential stages managed by the orchestration engine.
childStories.description:
The pipeline consists of sequential stages managed by the orchestration engine.

Each stage produces artifacts that become inputs for the next stage.
acceptanceCriteria: The Pipeline Overview behavior is implemented according to the codex definition.
The implementation covers these codex details: Idea Approved, ↓, Project Initialization.
Dependencies and integration points with the rest of PROJECT GENERATION PIPELINE are documented.

devNotes.rawReference:
The pipeline consists of sequential stages managed by the orchestration engine.

``` id="pgp-1"
Idea Approved
      ↓
Project Initialization
      ↓
Architecture Design
      ↓
Technology Stack Selection
      ↓
Repository Creation
      ↓
Component Assembly
      ↓
Code Generation
      ↓
Testing
      ↓
Documentation
      ↓
Deployment Configuration
      ↓
Project Publication
```

Each stage produces artifacts that become inputs for the next stage.

---

```
                              │
                              ▼
                        Agent Plugins
```

Everything runs locally.

stories.heading: Risk Score Model
stories.title: [Story] RISK ANALYSIS AND COMPLIANCE SCORING: Risk Score Model
stories.userStory: As a product owner, I want the risk score model capability, so that risk scoring works inversely.
stories.description:
Risk scoring works inversely.

Higher risk reduces the final score.
acceptanceCriteria: The Risk Score Model behavior is implemented according to the codex definition.
The implementation covers these codex details: Risk Penalty = 0 – 40, Final Score = Idea Score − Risk Penalty, Idea Score: 75.
Dependencies and integration points with the rest of RISK ANALYSIS AND COMPLIANCE SCORING are documented.

technicalDetails: Final Score = Idea Score − Risk Penalty
devNotes.rawReference:
Risk scoring works inversely.

Higher risk reduces the final score.

```
Risk Penalty = 0 – 40
```

Final idea score becomes:

```
Final Score = Idea Score − Risk Penalty
```

Example:

```
Idea Score: 75
Risk Penalty: 18
Final Score: 57
Decision: DEFER
```

stories.title: [Story] SECURITY AND COMPLIANCE VALIDATION SYSTEM: Risk Scoring System
stories.userStory: As a product owner, I want the risk scoring system capability, so that each project receives a combined risk score.
stories.description:
Each project receives a combined risk score.

Score categories include:
acceptanceCriteria: The Risk Scoring System behavior is implemented according to the codex definition.
The implementation covers these codex details: security risk, legal risk, privacy risk.
Dependencies and integration points with the rest of SECURITY AND COMPLIANCE VALIDATION SYSTEM are documented.

devNotes.rawReference:
Each project receives a combined risk score.

Score categories include:

```text
security risk
legal risk
privacy risk
ethical risk
operational risk
```

Example scoring model:

```text
0–25  Low risk
26–50 Moderate risk
51–75 High risk
76–100 Critical risk
```

Projects above a configured threshold cannot be deployed automatically.

stories.userStory: As a product owner, I want the safety controls capability, so that humans must be able to quickly stop unsafe behavior.
stories.description:
Humans must be able to quickly stop unsafe behavior.

Emergency controls include:
acceptanceCriteria: The Safety Controls behavior is implemented according to the codex definition.
The implementation covers these codex details: global task pause, agent shutdown, cluster task cancellation.
Dependencies and integration points with the rest of HUMAN COLLABORATION AND OVERRIDE SYSTEM are documented.

devNotes.rawReference:
Humans must be able to quickly stop unsafe behavior.

Emergency controls include:

```text
global task pause
agent shutdown
cluster task cancellation
project quarantine
```

These controls protect system integrity.

devNotes.rawReference:


This makes the system useful for both beginners and experts.
